# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

properties:
  zone: us-central1-a
  machineType: e2-medium
  image: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-11
  instanceNamePrefix: quick-instance
  instanceCount: 1

resources:
{% for i in range(properties['instanceCount']) %}
- name: {{ properties['instanceNamePrefix'] }}-{{ i+1 }}
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: {{ properties['zone'] }}
    machineType: zones/{{ properties['zone'] }}/machineTypes/{{ properties['machineType'] }}
    disks:
    - deviceName: boot
      type: PERSISTENT
      boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: {{ properties['image'] }}
        diskSizeGb: 10
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      accessConfigs:
      - name: External NAT
        type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
    serviceAccounts:
    - email: default
      scopes:
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/devstorage.read_only
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/logging.write
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/monitoring.write
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/servicecontrol
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/service.management.readonly
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/trace.append
{% endfor %}
